race_name: L'Etape Australia by Tour de France

top_categories.workout_context: 93ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic L'Etape Australia by Tour de France's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before December.
At L'Etape Australia by Tour de France, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
L'Etape Australia by Tour de France's paved mountainous coastal roads -- fully closed forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 98 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of L'Etape Australia by Tour de France. Train it until it feels boring.
At mile 60 of 98, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
9,088ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
9,088ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Low-cadence force work for 9,088ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 98 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At L'Etape Australia by Tour de France, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 9,088ft of climbing on paved mountainous coastal roads -- fully closed does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for L'Etape Australia by Tour de France’s 98 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at L'Etape Australia by Tour de France requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on paved mountainous coastal roads -- fully closed course to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, Race Simulation, and Over Under to prepare you for 98 miles of paved mountainous coastal roads -- fully closed course in Wollongong to Kiama, New South Wales, Australia.
